Prerequisite for this guide is completion of the [AWS User Creation Guide](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bVgSodD6-sztRKshKu_xEwyjUxdQsLA1IpUGAA8z3fo/edit?usp=sharing). This guide walks you through setting up your project to have the trial developer role so that you can host the frontend locally and use the existing backend for development.   
If you have any questions feel free to ping me (Trevor)

1. Sign in to your aws account as the root user (no pictures, you got this\!\!\!)  
2. Search and go to IAM

![][image1]

3. Go to the IAM users tab

![][image2]

4. Go to your create TrailDev User

![][image3]

5. Click add permissions then click create inline policy

![][image4]

6. Select JSON then paste the following text

{  
    "Version": "2012-10-17",  
    "Statement": \[  
        {  
            "Effect": "Allow",  
            "Action": \[  
                "iam:CreateAccessKey",  
                "iam:ListAccessKeys",  
                "iam:DeleteAccessKey"  
            \],  
            "Resource": "arn:aws:iam::\*:user/${aws:username}"  
        }  
    \]  
}  
![][image5]

7. Scroll down and click next  
   ![][image6]  
8. Name the policy TrailDevAccessKey

![][image7]

9. Create the policy  
   ![][image8]  
10. Click on your name in the upper right corner

![][image9]

11. Click sign out

![][image10]

12. Sign in to console

![][image11]

13. Enter correct information for the TrailDev user

![][image12]

14. Go to [this link](https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home?region=us-east-1#/security_credentials)

![][image13]

15. Scroll down to access keys and click Create Access Key

![][image14]

16. Select the Command Line Interface use case

![][image15]

17. Check the I understand box then click next

![][image16]

18. Click Create access key

![][image17]

19. Keep note of the access key and secret access key (download the csv if you want), then hit done

![][image18]

20. Install the aws cli from [this link](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/getting-started-install.html)  
21. On your cloned repository in the terminal run aws configure and enter your keys

![][image19]

22. Under Users/\<YourUser\>/.aws you will find config and credentials, open them both  
23. Add the below text to the config file

\[profile dev-role\]  
role\_arn \= arn:aws:iam::650244845886:role/Trail\_Mgr\_Dev  
source\_profile \= TrailDev   
region \= us-east-1  
![][image20]

24. Add the below text to the credentials file (your keys will be the same since you configured with the user key (i (Trevor) didn’t))

\[TrailDev\]  
aws\_access\_key\_id \= AKIAQSOI4H44TPGQYOUH  
aws\_secret\_access\_key \= GfuZNbtU4/tQxPlgcRaFY/yPxuo5R4dV50qFG/oH  
![][image21]  
Now when you run npm run dev it will call the setup\_env.py script to retrieve the latest aws id’s and url’s for our aws stuff  
(note these steps can be completed anytime, but the script and such haven’t been put in the repo at the time of writing so this won’t actually do anything until that happens)
